additionalPlain:
About us 
Welcome to Meesho, where every story begins with a spark of inspiration and a dash of entrepreneurial spirit. We're not just a platform; we're your partner in turning dreams into realities.
Curious about life at Meesho? Explore our Glassdoor - our people have a lot to say and they've helped us become a loved workplace in India.
Our Mission
Democratising internet commerce for everyone — Meesho (Meri Shop) started with a single idea in mind: to be an e-commerce destination for Indian consumers and to enable small businesses to succeed online.
We provide our sellers with benefits such as zero commission and affordable shipping solutions in the market. Today, sellers nationwide are growing their businesses by tapping into Meesho’s large and diverse customer base, state-of-the-art tech infrastructure, and pan-India logistics network through trusted third-party partners.
Affordable, relatable merchandise that mirrors local markets has helped us connect with internet users and serve customers across urban, semi-urban, and rural India. Our unique business model and continuous innovation have established us as a part of India’s e-commerce ecosystem.
Culture and Total Rewards
Our focus is on cultivating a dynamic workplace characterized by high impact and performance excellence. We prioritize a people-centric culture, dedicated to hiring and developing exceptional talent.
Total rewards at Meesho comprise a comprehensive set of elements — monetary, non-monetary, tangible, and intangible. Our 9 guiding principles, or "Mantras," are the backbone of how we operate, influencing everything from recognition and evaluation to growth discussions. Daily rituals and processes like “Problem First Mindset,” “Listen or Die,” our Internal Mobility Program, Talent Reviews, and Continuous Performance Management embody these principles.
We offer competitive compensation — both cash and equity-based — tailored to job roles, individual experience, and skill, along with employee-centric benefits and a supportive work environment. Our holistic wellness program, MeeCare, includes benefits across physical, mental, financial, and social wellness. This includes extensive medical insurance for employees and their families, wellness initiatives like telehealth, wellness events, and fitness-related perks.
To support work-life balance, we offer generous leave policies, parental support, retirement benefits, and learning and development assistance. Through personalized recognition, gratitude for stretched work, and engaging activities, we promote employee delight at the workplace. Additional benefits such as salary advance support, relocation assistance, and flexible benefit plans further enrich the Meesho experience.
At Meesho, we are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace where every individual can thrive. In compliance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, we uphold the following principles:
Equal Opportunity: We ensure that employment opportunities are never denied on the grounds of disability if the candidate is otherwise competent to perform the job.
Accessible Workplace: Our facilities are designed to be fully accessible, with amenities and assistive devices provided to support differently abled individuals in their work.
Inclusive Hiring Process: We adopt a transparent and non-discriminatory selection process, including providing application forms in alternate formats and offering reasonable accommodations during interviews upon request.
Career Growth: We provide adequate training post-recruitment and pre-promotion, with training materials available in accessible formats to enable equal career progression.
Support & Confidentiality: A dedicated liaison officer/committee addresses concerns and grievances, while maintaining strict confidentiality of disability-related information.
Awareness & Inclusion: We conduct awareness programs to promote a culture of inclusivity across the organization.
Meesho welcomes applicants and employees of all abilities and is dedicated to fostering an environment where differently abled persons can achieve their full potential.
Know more about Meesho here : https://www.meesho.io/

descriptionPlain:
About the Team

Would you like to be part of creating a unicorn within a unicorn? Solve some of the biggest challenges facing financial inclusion of Bharat? Well, then here’s your chance!
Financial Services (FS) is one of the biggest new bets we are taking at Meesho on the next 10x horizon opportunity for us. Just as Ant Financial did for Alibaba, and Mercado Pago did for Mercado Libre, our vision is to build financial services for Meesho, becoming our key growth and revenue driver.
We have found our Product-Market Fit and built and proved 0-1 and 1-10 journeys, Now looking for someone who can be the founding member of 10-100-1000 journey and this is possibly one of the most exciting and enriching experiences one could consider in their career.
As a Financial Services team, we always look at the bottom line. And the bottom line is - in this team, you should expect to have fun as you learn and grow.
 
About the Role
 
Lending is going to be our primary focus in this phase of our FS journey, and risk management is at the core of building any sustainable credit business at scale. Herein also lies potentially our biggest challenge for Meesho users: How do we assess risk of borrowers without any credit history? How can we leverage alternative information to better profile borrowers? How can we leverage our platform uniquely to create a sustained competitive advantage?
The Person will be responsible for driving the Policy, DS and analytics behind all these questions, from creating analytical frameworks, developing high quality analysis, driving insights and actions, and owning policy decisions.
In the early phases, your role will focus on refining initially implemented policy by Analyzing and Designing credit policies using Meesho platform data, bureau data, alternate data. Creating Scorecards and models for approval. 
Designing experiments to test cohort behavior and experimental policies.
Automate reporting and tracking of the portfolio on multiple levels , Ops performance measures, key performance indicators and associated drivers.

lists.content:
• Over the longer horizon, as the program scales you are expected to do the following:
  • Owning an automated decisioning process and its outcome.
  • Creating 6 Month Risk  roadmap
  • Refine the Credit policies and create new policies to lend in unexplored segments.
  • Own end to end ML model development methodology : Feature creation, Model design and development, tuning, validating, monitoring and calibrating
  • Coding up and maintaining BRE , automating credit policies as well as underwriting decisions if need be
  • Independently drive and conceptualize projects on Risk and Data science.
  • Initially an IC role but expected to guide and manage a team of analysts and grow into leadership role.

• 3-6  years of relevant Fin-Tech experience, preferably in Digital Unsecured Lending, with products like  BNPL, PL or consumer loan.
  • Strong RCA skills along with excellent critical thinking skills, combined with the ability to present your beliefs clearly and compellingly, both verbally and in written form to leadership.
  • Working knowledge of GCP, Vertex AI and  cloud function for deployment.
  • Solid understanding of credit bureau data (CIBIL, Equifax, CRIF), and banking risk frameworks.
  • Expert in PD, EAD , LGD modeling in Python environment. 
  • Can identify use cases of AI to drive productivity internally in the team and in the decisioning process.
    •

Curious about life at Meesho? Explore our Glassdoor - our people have a lot to say and they've helped us become a loved workplace in India.
Our Mission
Democratising internet commerce for everyone — Meesho (Meri Shop) started with a single idea in mind: to be an e-commerce destination for Indian consumers and to enable small businesses to succeed online.
We provide our sellers with benefits such as zero commission and affordable shipping solutions in the market. Today, sellers nationwide are growing their businesses by tapping into Meesho’s large and diverse customer base, state-of-the-art tech infrastructure, and pan-India logistics network through trusted third-party partners.
Affordable, relatable merchandise that mirrors local markets has helped us connect with internet users and serve customers across urban, semi-urban, and rural India. Our unique business model and continuous innovation have established us as a part of India’s e-commerce ecosystem.
Culture and Total Rewards
Our focus is on cultivating a dynamic workplace characterized by high impact and performance excellence. We prioritize a people-centric culture, dedicated to hiring and developing exceptional talent.

lists.content:
• Define and evolve the long-term Android architecture strategy, including modularization and migration paths away from legacy stacks

  • Drive cross-team architectural decisions and write ADRs that align mobile and platform teams on shared patterns

  • Lead architectural reviews, deep-dive design sessions, and refactoring of high-impact surfaces

  • Set engineering standards for code quality, testing strategy, and release readiness

  • Own end-to-end app performance — startup, jank, memory, ANRs, battery.

  • Champion AI-assisted developer workflows — Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, AI-driven code review and test generation and measure productivity impact. 

  • Identify and lead 2–3 crucial strategic projects per year from problem framing through rollout. 

  • Build the next generation of internal SDKs, design system components, and platform libraries consumed by other Android engineers.

  • Recruit, mentor, and grow Android engineers; partner on hiring loops and career growth

  • Perform deep code reviews that raise the technical bar

  • Represent Android in cross-functional planning and shape the broader mobile roadmap

• 8+ years of Android development experience, with at least 2 shipped production apps

  • Strong command of Jetpack Compose, state management, and the modern Android UI toolkit

  • Hands-on experience with the modern Jetpack stack: Hilt, Room, DataStore, WorkManager, Navigation, Paging

  • Solid understanding of architecture patterns — unidirectional data flow, MVI, clean architecture — and how to make them testable

  • Proven debugging skills — memory leaks, ANRs, jank, cold-start regressions.

  • Experience with performance tooling: Macrobenchmark, Baseline Profiles, App Startup, and R8 optimization

  • Proficiency with modern networking and serialization (Retrofit + OkHttp, kotlinx.serialization or Moshi) and image loading.

  • Strong grasp of concurrency, structured concurrency with Coroutines, and reactive streams.

  • Experience with modularization at scale, Gradle build optimization (KSP, configuration cache, build cache), and CI/CD pipelines

  • Comfort with AI-assisted development tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) in day-to-day workflow

  • Understanding of advanced Android internals — custom Compose layouts, background execution constraints, process lifecycle, security best practices

  • Strong Git, code review, and collaboration habits

descriptionPlain:
About the Team
The BizFin team at Meesho is a close-knit and high-performing team comprising professionals from Big 4 consulting firms, FMCG companies, and leading investment firms. The team includes Chartered Accountants, business specialists, and former investment bankers, each bringing diverse expertise and perspectives to the table.
The team focuses on solving complex and high-impact business problems across Meesho’s ecosystem. From driving growth strategies to improving financial operations, BizFin plays a crucial role in influencing key business decisions and contributing to the company’s success.
The team also emphasizes cross-functional exposure, enabling members to work across multiple business areas and gain broader learning opportunities. At its core, the team values excellence, collaboration, and strong team relationships, fostering a culture that celebrates both individual growth and collective impact.
About the Role
As AM - Finance, you will be responsible for developing and maintaining robust financial plans and ensuring effective execution. You will play a key role in driving financial planning, budgeting, and analysis to support evolving business requirements.
You will also be expected to adapt financial plans and budgets in line with changing business strategies and priorities. On a day-to-day basis, the role involves providing financial support and strategic advice to management while assisting in closing business deals.
This role offers an opportunity to work on impactful finance-related challenges at one of India’s leading e-commerce platforms, contributing to initiatives that affect millions of users across the country.

The team focuses on solving complex and high-impact business problems across Meesho’s ecosystem. From driving growth strategies to improving financial operations, BizFin plays a crucial role in influencing key business decisions and contributing to the company’s success.

The team also emphasizes cross-functional exposure, enabling members to work across multiple business areas and gain broader learning opportunities. At its core, the team values excellence, collaboration, and strong team relationships, fostering a culture that celebrates both individual growth and collective impact.

About the Role

As AM - Finance, you will be responsible for developing and maintaining robust financial plans and ensuring effective execution. You will play a key role in driving financial planning, budgeting, and analysis to support evolving business requirements.

You will also be expected to adapt financial plans and budgets in line with changing business strategies and priorities. On a day-to-day basis, the role involves providing financial support and strategic advice to management while assisting in closing business deals.

This role offers an opportunity to work on impactful finance-related challenges at one of India’s leading e-commerce platforms, contributing to initiatives that affect millions of users across the country.

lists.content:
• Work closely with specific business units and actively participate in its operations and management

  • Jointly own the OKRs of the respective business units and build an AOP to achieve them

  • Review the plans vs. actuals on a regular basis for the business you are associated with.

  • Complete account-level month-end closing reviews, procedures, and corrections as needed.

  • Handle ad hoc requests and problem-solving with a short turnaround time

  • Participate and drive standardisation automation projects for process efficiency.

  • People management – internal stakeholder management

  • Critical attributes to success would be strong communication, cadence and resilience.

• Ability to deal with ambiguity and ever-changing needs.

  • Strong analytical rigour and problem-solving skills.

  • Qualified CA Fresher from 2025/2026 batch.

additionalPlain:
About us 
Welcome to Meesho, where every story begins with a spark of inspiration and a dash of entrepreneurial spirit. We're not just a platform; we're your partner in turning dreams into realities.
Curious about life at Meesho? Explore our Glassdoor - our people have a lot to say and they've helped us become a loved workplace in India.
Our Mission
Democratising internet commerce for everyone — Meesho (Meri Shop) started with a single idea in mind: to be an e-commerce destination for Indian consumers and to enable small businesses to succeed online.
We provide our sellers with benefits such as zero commission and affordable shipping solutions in the market. Today, sellers nationwide are growing their businesses by tapping into Meesho’s large and diverse customer base, state-of-the-art tech infrastructure, and pan-India logistics network through trusted third-party partners.
Affordable, relatable merchandise that mirrors local markets has helped us connect with internet users and serve customers across urban, semi-urban, and rural India. Our unique business model and continuous innovation have established us as a part of India’s e-commerce ecosystem.
Culture and Total Rewards
Our focus is on cultivating a dynamic workplace characterized by high impact and performance excellence. We prioritize a people-centric culture, dedicated to hiring and developing exceptional talent.
Total rewards at Meesho comprise a comprehensive set of elements — monetary, non-monetary, tangible, and intangible. Our 9 guiding principles, or "Mantras," are the backbone of how we operate, influencing everything from recognition and evaluation to growth discussions. Daily rituals and processes like “Problem First Mindset,” “Listen or Die,” our Internal Mobility Program, Talent Reviews, and Continuous Performance Management embody these principles.
We offer competitive compensation — both cash and equity-based — tailored to job roles, individual experience, and skill, along with employee-centric benefits and a supportive work environment. Our holistic wellness program, MeeCare, includes benefits across physical, mental, financial, and social wellness. This includes extensive medical insurance for employees and their families, wellness initiatives like telehealth, wellness events, and fitness-related perks.
To support work-life balance, we offer generous leave policies, parental support, retirement benefits, and learning and development assistance. Through personalized recognition, gratitude for stretched work, and engaging activities, we promote employee delight at the workplace. Additional benefits such as salary advance support, relocation assistance, and flexible benefit plans further enrich the Meesho experience.
At Meesho, we are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace where every individual can thrive. In compliance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, we uphold the following principles:
Equal Opportunity: We ensure that employment opportunities are never denied on the grounds of disability if the candidate is otherwise competent to perform the job.
Accessible Workplace: Our facilities are designed to be fully accessible, with amenities and assistive devices provided to support differently abled individuals in their work.
Inclusive Hiring Process: We adopt a transparent and non-discriminatory selection process, including providing application forms in alternate formats and offering reasonable accommodations during interviews upon request.
Career Growth: We provide adequate training post-recruitment and pre-promotion, with training materials available in accessible formats to enable equal career progression.
Support & Confidentiality: A dedicated liaison officer/committee addresses concerns and grievances, while maintaining strict confidentiality of disability-related information.
Awareness & Inclusion: We conduct awareness programs to promote a culture of inclusivity across the organization.
Meesho welcomes applicants and employees of all abilities and is dedicated to fostering an environment where differently abled persons can achieve their full potential.
Know more about Meesho here : https://www.meesho.io/

descriptionPlain:
About the Team
You will be part of the Seller Growth team, responsible for driving the end-to-end seller lifecycle—from acquisition and activation to retention and scale. The team focuses on onboarding high-quality sellers, improving their early success, and enabling sustained business growth on the platform.
Operating in a fast-paced, high-ownership environment, the team combines structured problem-solving with rapid execution to unlock supply-side growth at scale.
About the Role
We are looking for a Assistant Manager– Performance Marketing to join our Seller Growth team.
In this role, you will own and scale seller acquisition and activation through performance marketing channels such as Google, Meta, and emerging platforms. You will focus on driving high-quality seller onboarding, improving activation rates, and optimizing CAC and payback periods.
This role requires a strong analytical mindset, experimentation-led thinking, and the ability to translate data into actionable growth strategies.
description:
About the Team

You will be part of the Seller Growth team, responsible for driving the end-to-end seller lifecycle—from acquisition and activation to retention and scale. The team focuses on onboarding high-quality sellers, improving their early success, and enabling sustained business growth on the platform.

Operating in a fast-paced, high-ownership environment, the team combines structured problem-solving with rapid execution to unlock supply-side growth at scale.

About the Role

We are looking for a Assistant Manager– Performance Marketing to join our Seller Growth team.

In this role, you will own and scale seller acquisition and activation through performance marketing channels such as Google, Meta, and emerging platforms. You will focus on driving high-quality seller onboarding, improving activation rates, and optimizing CAC and payback periods.

This role requires a strong analytical mindset, experimentation-led thinking, and the ability to translate data into actionable growth strategies.

lists.content:
Seller Acquisition & Channel Ownership

  • Own and scale seller acquisition across performance marketing channels (Google, Meta, affiliates, etc.)

  • Drive high-quality seller sign-ups with a focus on downstream activation and retention

  • Optimize campaigns for cost-efficient acquisition (CAC) while maintaining seller quality benchmarks 

    • Funnel Optimization (Signup → Activation → First Success)

  • Improve seller funnel metrics such as signup → KYC completion → catalog upload → first order

  • Identify and reduce drop-offs across onboarding journeys

  • Work closely with product and lifecycle teams to improve conversion rates 

    • Experimentation & Growth

  • Run structured experiments across audiences, creatives, landing pages, and funnel interventions

  • Identify new growth levers such as regional expansion, category-specific acquisition, and vernacular targeting

  • Test and scale new channels, formats, and campaign strategies 

    • Performance & Efficiency Management

  • Monitor and optimize key metrics such as CAC, activation rate, payback period, LTV/CAC, CVR, CTR, CPM

  • Manage budget allocation across channels to maximize incremental seller growth

  • Track and mitigate issues like audience saturation, creative fatigue, and declining marginal returns

    • Insights & Analytics

  • Analyze seller cohorts by category, geography, quality, and lifecycle stage

  • Derive insights on seller behavior to improve targeting and messaging

  • Build frameworks for incrementality and attribution for seller acquisition 

    • Cross-functional Collaboration

  • Partner with product, analytics, design, and category teams to align growth efforts

  • Influence onboarding experience, landing pages, and communication strategy based on performance insights

• 3+ years of experience in performance marketing, preferably in consumer internet, e-commerce, or app-based businesses

  • Strong hands-on expertise in Meta Ads & Google Ads, along with a solid understanding of incrementality frameworks

  • Strong understanding of key growth and performance metrics such as CPM, CTR, CTI, hook rate, CAC, reach, frequency, and attribution models

  • Proficiency in Excel/Google Sheets; working knowledge of SQL or analytics tools is a plus

  • Familiarity with seller or partner acquisition funnels is a plus

  • Strong analytical thinking with a structured, problem-first approach and a bias for action

  • Ability to operate with high ownership in a fast-paced environment

  • A strong growth mindset with a passion for experimentation, learning, and continuous improvement

  • Clear and effective communication skills, with the ability to work cross-functionally and collaboratively

Our focus is on cultivating a dynamic workplace characterized by high impact and performance excellence. We prioritize a people-centric culture, dedicated to hiring and developing exceptional talent.

This includes extensive medical insurance for employees and their families, wellness initiatives like telehealth, wellness events, and fitness-related perks.

descriptionPlain:
About Your Team
 
Our Human Resources team operates as a strategic partner to the business, focused on cultivating a high-performance culture where exceptional talent thrives. Our work is fundamentally guided by the DICE (Democratize Internet Commerce for Everyone) mission, which underpins our approach to all people strategies. Our core mandate centers on two critical pillars: driving world-class Talent Density and continuously elevating Employee Experience. We achieve this by relentlessly attracting, developing, and retaining the highest caliber of professionals, ensuring that every individual contributes significantly to our success. Concurrently, we are committed to shaping an unparalleled employee experience—one defined by transparent communication, meaningful development, and a deeply engaging, inclusive culture. As stewards of the company's most valuable asset—its people—we design, implement, and champion people strategies that directly translate into sustained business growth and a position as one of India’s most sought-after e-commerce destinations.

About The Role
We are looking for a highly driven and technically strong HR professional to join our team as an
Assistant Manager – HR, with a core focus on HR systems, analytics, AI-driven solutions, and
automation.
This role is not about managing processes at the surface level, it is about deep problem
discovery, building scalable solutions, and continuously improving the employee experience
through technology. The ideal candidate is someone who is curious, hands-on, and user-first,
with the ability to break down ambiguous problems and solve them using data, systems, and
emerging technologies like AI.
description:
About Your Team
 
Our Human Resources team operates as a strategic partner to the business, focused on cultivating a high-performance culture where exceptional talent thrives. Our work is fundamentally guided by the DICE (Democratize Internet Commerce for Everyone) mission, which underpins our approach to all people strategies. Our core mandate centers on two critical pillars: driving world-class Talent Density and continuously elevating Employee Experience. We achieve this by relentlessly attracting, developing, and retaining the highest caliber of professionals, ensuring that every individual contributes significantly to our success. Concurrently, we are committed to shaping an unparalleled employee experience—one defined by transparent communication, meaningful development, and a deeply engaging, inclusive culture. As stewards of the company's most valuable asset—its people—we design, implement, and champion people strategies that directly translate into sustained business growth and a position as one of India’s most sought-after e-commerce destinations.

About The Role

We are looking for a highly driven and technically strong HR professional to join our team as an
Assistant Manager – HR, with a core focus on HR systems, analytics, AI-driven solutions, and
automation.
This role is not about managing processes at the surface level, it is about deep problem
discovery, building scalable solutions, and continuously improving the employee experience
through technology. The ideal candidate is someone who is curious, hands-on, and user-first,
with the ability to break down ambiguous problems and solve them using data, systems, and
emerging technologies like AI.

lists.content:
1. HR Systems Ownership (SuccessFactors & HR Tech Stack)
● Lead the implementation and management of SAP SuccessFactors modules (implementation, configuration, enhancements, and adoption)
● Act as the functional and technical owner of the HR tech stack (HRMS, LMS, integrations)
● Continuously evaluate and improve system architecture to ensure scalability and efficiency
● Troubleshoot issues hands-on and drive resolution with vendors/IT teams

2. AI-Driven HR Transformation
● Identify and implement AI use cases in HR (e.g., predictive analytics, chatbot automation, intelligent workflows)
● Leverage AI tools to improve decision-making, employee experience, and operational efficiency
● Experiment with emerging tools (preferably LLMs like Gemini, ChatGPT, etc.) and translate them into real HR applications
● Drive a culture of AI-first thinking within HR operations

3. Problem Discovery & Solutioning (User-First Approach)
● Go beyond stated requirements to identify root problems and inefficiencies
● Apply first-principles thinking to redesign HR processes
● Build solutions with a strong focus on employee experience and usability
● Partner with stakeholders to deeply understand pain points and translate them into scalable tech solutions

4. HR Analytics & Data Intelligence
● Build advanced dashboards, reports, and predictive models for HR metrics (attrition, hiring funnel, performance, enps)
● Work with large datasets, including data extraction, transformation, and analysis
● Enable data-driven decision-making across HR and leadership teams
● Bring storytelling into data, convert insights into actionable narratives

5. Automation & Workflow Optimization
● Identify and eliminate manual processes through end-to-end automation
● Build workflows using tools like Power Automate, Zapier, scripting (Python/SQL), or similar
● Design self-serve HR solutions for employees and managers
● Continuously improve turnaround time, accuracy, and scalability of HR operations

6. Integrations & API Management
● Design and manage system integrations between tools
● Work with APIs, webhooks, and middleware tools for seamless data exchange
● Collaborate with other BUs, such as IT, Finance, InfoSec, and CREWS to ensure robust, secure, and scalable integrations

7. Governance, Risk & Compliance
● Ensure compliance with ITGC (IT General Controls) and audit frameworks
● Maintain strong controls around data privacy, access management, and system integrity
● Support internal and external audits with proper documentation and system readiness

8. Stakeholder Collaboration & Enablement
● Work closely with HR, IT, Finance, InfoSec, and CREWS teams.
● Translate business needs into technical and system solutions
● Drive adoption through training, documentation, and change management

Experience:
● 3–6 years in HR Operations / HR Tech / People Analytics
● Hands-on experience with SAP SuccessFactors (implementation/configuration is a must)

Technical Skills:
● Strong in HR analytics (Advanced Excel, SQL, BI tools like Power BI/Tableau)
● Hands-on experience with automation tools and scripting
● Working knowledge of APIs, integrations, and system architecture
● Practical exposure to AI tools and applications in HR
● Strong understanding of data structures and workflows

Other Skills:
● Strong stakeholder management and communication
● High ownership and accountability
● Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment

text: Assistant Manager – HR (HR Systems, Analytics & AI)

discovery, building scalable solutions, and continuously improving the employee experience
through technology. The ideal candidate is someone who is curious, hands-on, and user-first,
with the ability to break down ambiguous problems and solve them using data, systems, and
emerging technologies like AI.

additionalPlain:
About us 
Welcome to Meesho, where every story begins with a spark of inspiration and a dash of entrepreneurial spirit. We're not just a platform; we're your partner in turning dreams into realities.
Curious about life at Meesho? Explore our Glassdoor - our people have a lot to say and they've helped us become a loved workplace in India.
Our Mission
Democratising internet commerce for everyone — Meesho (Meri Shop) started with a single idea in mind: to be an e-commerce destination for Indian consumers and to enable small businesses to succeed online.
We provide our sellers with benefits such as zero commission and affordable shipping solutions in the market. Today, sellers nationwide are growing their businesses by tapping into Meesho’s large and diverse customer base, state-of-the-art tech infrastructure, and pan-India logistics network through trusted third-party partners.
Affordable, relatable merchandise that mirrors local markets has helped us connect with internet users and serve customers across urban, semi-urban, and rural India. Our unique business model and continuous innovation have established us as a part of India’s e-commerce ecosystem.
Culture and Total Rewards
Our focus is on cultivating a dynamic workplace characterized by high impact and performance excellence. We prioritize a people-centric culture, dedicated to hiring and developing exceptional talent.
Total rewards at Meesho comprise a comprehensive set of elements — monetary, non-monetary, tangible, and intangible. Our 9 guiding principles, or "Mantras," are the backbone of how we operate, influencing everything from recognition and evaluation to growth discussions. Daily rituals and processes like “Problem First Mindset,” “Listen or Die,” our Internal Mobility Program, Talent Reviews, and Continuous Performance Management embody these principles.
We offer competitive compensation — both cash and equity-based — tailored to job roles, individual experience, and skill, along with employee-centric benefits and a supportive work environment. Our holistic wellness program, MeeCare, includes benefits across physical, mental, financial, and social wellness. This includes extensive medical insurance for employees and their families, wellness initiatives like telehealth, wellness events, and fitness-related perks.
To support work-life balance, we offer generous leave policies, parental support, retirement benefits, and learning and development assistance. Through personalized recognition, gratitude for stretched work, and engaging activities, we promote employee delight at the workplace. Additional benefits such as salary advance support, relocation assistance, and flexible benefit plans further enrich the Meesho experience.
At Meesho, we are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace where every individual can thrive. In compliance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, we uphold the following principles:
Equal Opportunity: We ensure that employment opportunities are never denied on the grounds of disability if the candidate is otherwise competent to perform the job.
Accessible Workplace: Our facilities are designed to be fully accessible, with amenities and assistive devices provided to support differently abled individuals in their work.
Inclusive Hiring Process: We adopt a transparent and non-discriminatory selection process, including providing application forms in alternate formats and offering reasonable accommodations during interviews upon request.
Career Growth: We provide adequate training post-recruitment and pre-promotion, with training materials available in accessible formats to enable equal career progression.
Support & Confidentiality: A dedicated liaison officer/committee addresses concerns and grievances, while maintaining strict confidentiality of disability-related information.
Awareness & Inclusion: We conduct awareness programs to promote a culture of inclusivity across the organization.
Meesho welcomes applicants and employees of all abilities and is dedicated to fostering an environment where differently abled persons can achieve their full potential.
Know more about Meesho here : https://www.meesho.io/

descriptionPlain:
About the Role

We are looking for a AM – Finance to assist in Books, Audit, Banking, Insurance, and Assets at
Meesho. This role offers the chance to work on high-impact projects and contribute to building financial
processes that complies requirement of a listed company. You’ll ensure accurate reporting, smooth audits,
strong treasury management, and lead critical finance initiatives.
 
About the Team

At Meesho, you’ll be part of the core IPO journey, shaping financial processes that set new industry
benchmarks. This is your chance to grow with a company that’s redefining how India shops.
description:
About the Role

We are looking for a AM – Finance to assist in Books, Audit, Banking, Insurance, and Assets at
Meesho. This role offers the chance to work on high-impact projects and contribute to building financial
processes that complies requirement of a listed company. You’ll ensure accurate reporting, smooth audits,
strong treasury management, and lead critical finance initiatives.
 
About the Team

At Meesho, you’ll be part of the core IPO journey, shaping financial processes that set new industry
benchmarks. This is your chance to grow with a company that’s redefining how India shops.

lists.content:
• Prepare and ensure timely closure of financial statements (standalone & consolidated) in
    compliance with Ind AS and Indian GAAP, with high accuracy and completeness.
  • Independently evaluate and implement accounting treatment for new business initiatives and
    complex transactions, including appropriate disclosures in financial statements.
  • Execute financial reporting requirements, including audit readiness, documentation, and
    external reporting, aligned with listed company standards.
  • Manage end-to-end coordination with Big 4 auditors to ensure smooth and timely completion of
    quarterly and annual audits.
  • Drive month-end and year-end close processes, including consolidation, with a focus on
    timelines, accuracy, and process improvements.
  • Prepare and support deliverables for quarterly reviews, annual reports, and investor queries,
    ensuring high-quality outputs.
  • Assist in treasury operations, including cash flow monitoring, banking coordination, investments
    tracking, and support on hedging execution.
  • Oversee accounting operations (AP, AR, GL, payroll), ensuring accuracy, controls, and
    adherence to timelines.
  • Manage fixed assets and insurance processes, ensuring compliance, tracking, and appropriate
    accounting.
  • Ensure adherence to internal controls, SOPs, and governance frameworks, and proactively
    drive process improvements and standardization.
  • Coordinate with external consultants/advisors to ensure alignment of accounting practices with
    industry standards.
  • Mentor and guide junior team members, ensuring quality and timely delivery of team outputs.

• Chartered Accountant with up to 1 years of post-qualification experience in a listed organization
    handling statutory reporting.
  • Solid knowledge of accounting standards, reporting, and ERP systems.
  • Experience in stakeholder management.
  • Strong execution bias, problem-solving skills, and ability to multitask.
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills.

descriptionPlain:
About the Team   
As part of our legal and secretarial team, you'll play a crucial role in supporting the Company with your experience in secretarial matters, corporate governance, IPO readiness, M&A.. Bring your skills and passion to a collaborative environment and play a vital role in shaping the company's journey towards excellence.
 
Our team believes in working hard and partying harder. We organize team trivia nights, board game marathons, and other fun events to foster our team spirit 💥.
 
About the Role
 
We are looking for a sharp, business-minded legal professional to join our Legal Team as Assistant Manager - Legal. This is a high-impact role at the intersection of law, business, and strategy - shaping key transactions, driving compliance excellence, and enabling the company’s growth journey. You will work closely with leadership and cross-functional stakeholders, acting as both a legal advisor and a business enabler.

Bring your skills and passion to a collaborative environment and play a vital role in shaping the company's journey towards excellence.
 
Our team believes in working hard and partying harder. We organize team trivia nights, board game marathons, and other fun events to foster our team spirit 💥.
 
About the Role
 
We are looking for a sharp, business-minded legal professional to join our Legal Team as Assistant Manager - Legal. This is a high-impact role at the intersection of law, business, and strategy - shaping key transactions, driving compliance excellence, and enabling the company’s growth journey. You will work closely with leadership and cross-functional stakeholders, acting as both a legal advisor and a business enabler.

lists.content:
• Lead the drafting, review, negotiation, and closure of a wide range of commercial agreements, including vendor, distribution, licensing, SaaS/technology, customer, procurement, and strategic partnership contracts.

  • Manage the end-to-end contracting process with enterprise customers, vendors, partners, and other external stakeholders, ensuring legal risks are appropriately managed while supporting business priorities and deal momentum. Commercial mindset - balancing legal risk with business opportunity.

  • Advise internal stakeholders on contract risks, obligations, liabilities, risk allocation, commercial terms, and other key legal and commercial considerations.

  • Partner closely with business, sales, finance, procurement, product, and operations teams to anticipate legal risks, structure transactions, and develop pragmatic solutions that help close deals responsibly.

  • Monitor regulatory developments, interpret implications for the business, and proactively recommend policy/process changes.

  • Drive continuous improvement in contract lifecycle management, compliance monitoring, and risk frameworks.

  • Strong foundation in contract and commercial laws, with proven experience in high-value contracts and transactions.

  • Exceptional drafting, negotiation, and communication skills.

  • Ability to distill complex legal issues into clear, actionable business advice.

  • Strategic thinker with sharp problem-solving skills and attention to detail.

  • Comfortable operating in fast-paced, high-growth environments with multiple priorities.

  • Effective stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and build trust across teams.

• Qualified LLB with 3-5 years of PQE.
  • Experience in a reputed law firm and/or in-house role with exposure to corporate advisory, transactions, and compliance.
  • Experience handling cross-border contracts and complex commercial negotiations.
  • Possess an analytical and strategic minds.
    •

descriptionPlain:
About the Team 

Meesho's Security & Compliance team safeguards a platform that 5% of Indian households shop with - millions of orders, billions of data points, zero downtime as a baseline. We own the Information Security Management System, drive every external certification, and shape how Meesho earns trust with sellers, buyers, partners and regulators. We move fast, default to automation, and obsess over evidence.
 
About the Role
This is a hands-on individual contributor role for someone who wants to drive - not just oversee - a multi-framework compliance program. You'll be the DRI for ISO 27001:2022 and SOC 2 Type II, run end-to-end ITGC and TPRM cycles, and help operationalise India's DPDP Rules 2025 across a product organisation that processes data at meaningful scale. You'll work directly with Engineering, IT, Legal, Product, and external auditors.
description:
About the Team 


Meesho's Security & Compliance team safeguards a platform that 5% of Indian households shop with - millions of orders, billions of data points, zero downtime as a baseline. We own the Information Security Management System, drive every external certification, and shape how Meesho earns trust with sellers, buyers, partners and regulators. We move fast, default to automation, and obsess over evidence.

 

About the Role

This is a hands-on individual contributor role for someone who wants to drive - not just oversee - a multi-framework compliance program. You'll be the DRI for ISO 27001:2022 and SOC 2 Type II, run end-to-end ITGC and TPRM cycles, and help operationalise India's DPDP Rules 2025 across a product organisation that processes data at meaningful scale. You'll work directly with Engineering, IT, Legal, Product, and external auditors.

lists.content:
• Certifications & external audits

  • Own the certification and surveillance cycle for ISO 27001:2022 and SOC 2 Type II; act as the single point of contact for external auditors.

  • Plan and execute readiness assessments, gap closure, evidence collection, control walkthroughs, and management responses.

  • Maintain audit calendars, evidence repositories, and bridge letters between audit windows.

  • Drive PCI DSS v4.0.1 scope-reduction and assessment activities for in-scope environments.

    • ISMS, policies & frameworks

  • Maintain Meesho's ISMS aligned to ISO 27001:2022 - all 93 Annex A controls mapped across Organizational, People, Physical and Technological themes, with named owners and live evidence.

  • Author, review, version-control and socialise security policies, standards, and procedures.

  • Map controls across frameworks: ISO 27001:2022, SOC 2 TSC, PCI DSS v4.0.1, NIST CSF 2.0, CIS Controls v8, DPDP.

    • ITGC & internal audits

  • Design, test and continuously improve IT General Controls: access management, change management, IT operations, and SDLC.

  • Plan and execute internal audits; track findings to closure with engineering and IT.

  • Build and maintain the enterprise risk register; run RCSA, define KRIs, drive risk treatment plans and residual-risk acceptance with leadership.

    • Third-Party Risk Management (TPRM)

  • Run the full vendor lifecycle: intake → tiering → security due diligence (SIG / CAIQ / SOC 2 / ISO reviews) → contractual controls → continuous monitoring → offboarding.

  • Partner with Legal and Procurement to embed security clauses in MSAs, DPAs, and sub-processor agreements.

  • Conduct on-site / virtual vendor audits for tier-1 vendors and report to the security council.

    • Privacy & data protection

  • Operationalise the DPDP Act 2023 + DPDP Rules 2025 across the business: DPIAs, consent and notice flows, data-principal rights, 72-hour breach notification, and Records of Processing Activity.

  • Prepare Meesho for likely Significant Data Fiduciary (SDF) obligations: independent data-auditor coordination, DPO interfacing, algorithmic transparency, and children's-data safeguards.

  • Track IT Act, CERT-In directions, and sector-specific guidelines as relevant.

    • Business continuity

  • Maintain BCP and DR aligned to ISO 22301 - BIAs, RTO/RPO definitions, and annual DR / failover testing.

    • Awareness & culture

  • Run organisation-wide security and privacy awareness: onboarding, refreshers, phishing simulations, and role-based modules.

    • Partner & customer trust

  • Respond to seller, partner and enterprise security questionnaires; maintain the Trust Center and security collateral.

• 4–6 years in security compliance, IT audit, or GRC at a product company (SaaS, fintech, e-commerce, payments, consumer internet).

  • Hands-on experience driving ISO 27001:2022 end-to-end: gap → implementation → certification → surveillance.

  • Hands-on experience driving SOC 2 Type II end-to-end, including auditor management.

  • Strong ITGC experience: access, change, ops, and SDLC control design and testing.

  • Strong TPRM experience across the full vendor lifecycle.

  • Working knowledge of cloud (AWS and/or GCP) - shared-responsibility model, CIS benchmarks, native services for evidence (AWS Config, GCP SCC, CloudTrail, IAM Analyzer).

  • Demonstrated stakeholder management with Engineering, IT, Legal, Product, and external auditors.

  • Excellent written communication - you'll author policies, audit responses, and risk reports read by senior leadership.

    • Nice to have

  • DPDP Act 2023 / DPDP Rules 2025 implementation experience; familiarity with GDPR or ISO 27701.

  • Hands-on with a GRC platform: Sprinto, Vanta, Drata, OneTrust, AuditBoard, MetricStream, ServiceNow GRC, or Archer.

  • ISO 22301 BCMS experience.

  • Exposure to RBI / SEBI / IRDAI sectoral compliance.

  • PCI DSS v4.0.1 experience.

    • Certifications

  • ISO 27001:2022 Lead Auditor / Lead Implementer

  • CISA

  • CIPP/E or DCPP (privacy)

Our holistic wellness program, MeeCare, includes benefits across physical, mental, financial, and social wellness. This includes extensive medical insurance for employees and their families, wellness initiatives like telehealth, wellness events, and fitness-related perks.
To support work-life balance, we offer generous leave policies, parental support, retirement benefits, and learning and development assistance. Through personalized recognition, gratitude for stretched work, and engaging activities, we promote employee delight at the workplace. Additional benefits such as salary advance support, relocation assistance, and flexible benefit plans further enrich the Meesho experience.
At Meesho, we are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace where every individual can thrive. In compliance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, we uphold the following principles:
Equal Opportunity: We ensure that employment opportunities are never denied on the grounds of disability if the candidate is otherwise competent to perform the job.
Accessible Workplace: Our facilities are designed to be fully accessible, with amenities and assistive devices provided to support differently abled individuals in their work.
Inclusive Hiring Process: We adopt a transparent and non-discriminatory selection process, including providing application forms in alternate formats and offering reasonable accommodations during interviews upon request.
Career Growth: We provide adequate training post-recruitment and pre-promotion, with training materials available in accessible formats to enable equal career progression.
Support & Confidentiality: A dedicated liaison officer/committee addresses concerns and grievances, while maintaining strict confidentiality of disability-related information.
Awareness & Inclusion: We conduct awareness programs to promote a culture of inclusivity across the organization.
Meesho welcomes applicants and employees of all abilities and is dedicated to fostering an environment where differently abled persons can achieve their full potential.
Know more about Meesho here : https://www.meesho.io/
additional:
About us 

Welcome to Meesho, where every story begins with a spark of inspiration and a dash of entrepreneurial spirit. We're not just a platform; we're your partner in turning dreams into realities.

Curious about life at Meesho? Explore our Glassdoor - our people have a lot to say and they've helped us become a loved workplace in India.

Our Mission

Democratising internet commerce for everyone — Meesho (Meri Shop) started with a single idea in mind: to be an e-commerce destination for Indian consumers and to enable small businesses to succeed online.

We provide our sellers with benefits such as zero commission and affordable shipping solutions in the market. Today, sellers nationwide are growing their businesses by tapping into Meesho’s large and diverse customer base, state-of-the-art tech infrastructure, and pan-India logistics network through trusted third-party partners.

Affordable, relatable merchandise that mirrors local markets has helped us connect with internet users and serve customers across urban, semi-urban, and rural India. Our unique business model and continuous innovation have established us as a part of India’s e-commerce ecosystem.

Culture and Total Rewards

Our focus is on cultivating a dynamic workplace characterized by high impact and performance excellence. We prioritize a people-centric culture, dedicated to hiring and developing exceptional talent.

Total rewards at Meesho comprise a comprehensive set of elements — monetary, non-monetary, tangible, and intangible. Our 9 guiding principles, or "Mantras," are the backbone of how we operate, influencing everything from recognition and evaluation to growth discussions. Daily rituals and processes like “Problem First Mindset,” “Listen or Die,” our Internal Mobility Program, Talent Reviews, and Continuous Performance Management embody these principles.

We offer competitive compensation — both cash and equity-based — tailored to job roles, individual experience, and skill, along with employee-centric benefits and a supportive work environment. Our holistic wellness program, MeeCare, includes benefits across physical, mental, financial, and social wellness. This includes extensive medical insurance for employees and their families, wellness initiatives like telehealth, wellness events, and fitness-related perks.

To support work-life balance, we offer generous leave policies, parental support, retirement benefits, and learning and development assistance. Through personalized recognition, gratitude for stretched work, and engaging activities, we promote employee delight at the workplace. Additional benefits such as salary advance support, relocation assistance, and flexible benefit plans further enrich the Meesho experience.

At Meesho, we are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace where every individual can thrive. In compliance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, we uphold the following principles:

  • Equal Opportunity: We ensure that employment opportunities are never denied on the grounds of disability if the candidate is otherwise competent to perform the job.

  • Accessible Workplace: Our facilities are designed to be fully accessible, with amenities and assistive devices provided to support differently abled individuals in their work.

  • Inclusive Hiring Process: We adopt a transparent and non-discriminatory selection process, including providing application forms in alternate formats and offering reasonable accommodations during interviews upon request.

  • Career Growth: We provide adequate training post-recruitment and pre-promotion, with training materials available in accessible formats to enable equal career progression.

  • Support & Confidentiality: A dedicated liaison officer/committee addresses concerns and grievances, while maintaining strict confidentiality of disability-related information.

  • Awareness & Inclusion: We conduct awareness programs to promote a culture of inclusivity across the organization.

Meesho welcomes applicants and employees of all abilities and is dedicated to fostering an environment where differently abled persons can achieve their full potential.

Know more about Meesho here : https://www.meesho.io/
descriptionPlain:
About the team
 
Would you like to be part of creating a unicorn within a unicorn? Solve some of the biggest challenges facing the financial inclusion of Bharat? Well, then here’s your chance! Financial Services (FS) is one of the fastest growing new businesses at Meesho. It's a large 10X opportunity for Meesho to build financial products for Bharat users and serve the unmet needs of millions of our users. And you will be at the center of this journey, building and scaling the business and serving millions of Meesho users. Meesho FS has several lines of products primarily in lending space, service sellers, users and logistics partners of Meesho.
We aspire to create one of the country’s largest financial services businesses, drawing inspiration from global giants such as Alibaba, Shopee, and Mercado Libre, who have thrived in markets comparable to India and built large financial businesses.
As part of this role, you will get to wear multiple hats, from strategy to execution, problem solving, to team and work closely with the leadership team to build and grow Meesho’s user lending business. You will also work closely with other cross functional teams such as Credit Risk, Product, Technology, User Growth and our external partners, including NBFCs and Banks. Moreover, you will be required to manage budgets and P&L with end to end responsibilities and ownership of managing the business. If you are looking for a high impact role, and a journey to cherish, this role is for you. 
 
About the role
 
In this role, you will be expected to lead one or more lending products, you will play a vital role in shaping the direction of the business and essentially own the P&L and success of the product. In addition you will own and manage partnerships with lenders and drive KRs on capital supply. You can expect to work on a wide range of problem statements spanning across business, product, P&L, team management and growth and create impact for our users and the organization.

About the team

 

Would you like to be part of creating a unicorn within a unicorn? Solve some of the biggest challenges facing the financial inclusion of Bharat? Well, then here’s your chance! Financial Services (FS) is one of the fastest growing new businesses at Meesho. It's a large 10X opportunity for Meesho to build financial products for Bharat users and serve the unmet needs of millions of our users. And you will be at the center of this journey, building and scaling the business and serving millions of Meesho users. Meesho FS has several lines of products primarily in lending space, service sellers, users and logistics partners of Meesho.

We aspire to create one of the country’s largest financial services businesses, drawing inspiration from global giants such as Alibaba, Shopee, and Mercado Libre, who have thrived in markets comparable to India and built large financial businesses.

As part of this role, you will get to wear multiple hats, from strategy to execution, problem solving, to team and work closely with the leadership team to build and grow Meesho’s user lending business. You will also work closely with other cross functional teams such as Credit Risk, Product, Technology, User Growth and our external partners, including NBFCs and Banks. Moreover, you will be required to manage budgets and P&L with end to end responsibilities and ownership of managing the business. If you are looking for a high impact role, and a journey to cherish, this role is for you. 

 

About the role

 

In this role, you will be expected to lead one or more lending products, you will play a vital role in shaping the direction of the business and essentially own the P&L and success of the product. In addition you will own and manage partnerships with lenders and drive KRs on capital supply. You can expect to work on a wide range of problem statements spanning across business, product, P&L, team management and growth and create impact for our users and the organization.

lists.content:
• Build and scale business:  develop a deep understanding of user needs for credit and build a differentiated product offering catering to different segments. This will need you to strategize and execute with a problem-first and impact mindset. You will be owning the strategy, building partnerships and execution roadmaps for your P&L

  • Growth: Grow the business, drive adoption, manage pricing and develop marketing strategies and positioning to increase awareness and adoptions on the platform. Take bold bets and ambitious targets for the business. 

  • Capital Supply: Create long term lender strategy, work closely with partnership team to bring in the right partners and ensure sufficient capital supply. 

  • P&L: Manage the overall P&L, identify levers across pricing and risk and manage them, monitor portfolio performance, collections and work closely with risk teams to identify and mitigate risks, 

  • Developing processes and systems:build systems and processes ground up, apply your problem solving that to drive continuous improvements

  • Team Management: manage the user business team, build KPIs/ targets and grow your team. Help them imbibe Meesho values and culture and enable them to be the best version of themselves.

  • Stakeholder Management - you will get to work closely with Meesho founders and the leadership team to build the business, align them on business direction and approvals, including budgets and P&L.

• About eight to twelve years of work experience managing a business growth and P&L  

  • Lending or fintech experience is preferred, 

  • Execution experience, demonstrated examples of delivering high impact projects 

  • Strong problem solving mindset and ability to break down complex complex problems, leverage data and come up with impactful solutions

  • Ability to aim big and take bold bets and work in agile fashion

  • Inquisitive mindset, continuous problem solving to improve and grow

  • Strong stakeholder management and communication skills

descriptionPlain:
About the Team:
 
The BizFin team at Meesho is a close-knit and high-performing group that brings together a diverse mix of professionals from Big 4 consulting, FMCG giants, and top-tier investment firms. Our members include Chartered Accountants (many of whom are rank holders), business specialists, and former investment bankers — each contributing a unique perspective and skill set.
 
We thrive on solving complex, high-impact business problems across Meesho’s ecosystem. Whether it's driving growth strategy or streamlining financial operations, the team plays a critical role in shaping key decisions that drive the company's success.
 
We believe in cross-functional exposure, ensuring team members gain experience across different areas of the business, enabling continuous learning and a broader perspective. At our core, we value both excellence in our work and strong team bonds, creating a culture that celebrates collaboration, achievement, and the collective impact we make.
 
About the Role: 
 
As Associate Director - Business Finance, you’ll strategise with the business team and tell us how and where to spend our money. While you ensure governance and controllership are best in class, more importantly you actively work with CXOs to drive prioritisation/ choices around growth and profitability. You will lead a team of expert finance professionals - who will aid you in problem discovery, implementing complex business solutions, playing the critical challenger role in business orgs and being co-pilot in leadership level decision-making. You’ll also manage business development activities according to a financial plan. You’ll modify financial plans and budget amounts based on the changes in business plans. You’ll also provide financial support and advice to management in closing business deals and short/medium/long term strategies.

lists.content:
• To work closely with the business unit and actively participate in its operations and management decision making
  • To develop financial plans along with various business and technology teams with rigor on bottom's up approach
  • To review the plans vs actuals on regular basis; in depth analysis of variance and communicating the same proactively creating right visibility and enabling planning of actionable
  • To track and measure key financials and business metrics
  • To jointly own the OKRs of the respective business unit and build an AOP to achieve the same. Completes account level month-end closing reviews, procedures, and corrections as needed
  • Directs and oversees the overall policies, objectives, and initiatives of the revenue cycle activities to optimize operations
  • Conduct periodic meetings with direct reports to assure staff understand assigned tasks and can manage time constraints
  • Do scenario analysis and develop business plans on product enhancement or business initiatives, present top management with analysis
  • Review the plans vs actuals on a regular basis; in-depth analysis of variance and communicating the same proactively creating right visibility and enabling planning of actionable
  • Handle Adhoc requests and problem-solving with a short turnaround time and supplement such requests with appropriate analysis and comments to support decision making
  • Participate & Drive standardization automation projects for process efficiency

• 6 to 10 years Exp, Chartered Accountant or Post-Graduate in Finance
  • Should hold at least 5 years of experience in Business Finance
  • Strong analytical rigor and problem-solving skills
  • Strong communication & Interpersonal skills
  • Proactive takes initiative, self-motivated, team player
  • Strong collaboration, stakeholder management and interpersonal skills
    •  
     
    (For candidates with non similar industry experience, the numbers of years of experience may vary.)

descriptionPlain:
Meesho is India’s fastest-growing internet commerce company, serving over 160 million customers.
As the competitive landscape in Indian e-commerce intensifies, we’re investing in building a world-
class strategic intelligence capability to sharpen how we allocate capital, set prices, plan long-range
strategy, and respond to market shifts.
We’re looking for a founding leader to build this function from the ground up. You will be responsible
for developing the systems, methodologies, and team that give Meesho’s leadership a continuously
updated, outside-in view of the competitive and market landscape — turning fragmented signals into
decision-grade insight.
This is a high-visibility, high-autonomy role. You will work directly with senior leadership and across
category, finance, and product teams. If you’ve spent years building expertise in market estimation,
data triangulation, and insight generation as a consultant or researcher, and you’re now ready to
own a function end-to-end inside a high-growth company — this role was designed for you
description:
Meesho is India’s fastest-growing internet commerce company, serving over 160 million customers.
As the competitive landscape in Indian e-commerce intensifies, we’re investing in building a world-
class strategic intelligence capability to sharpen how we allocate capital, set prices, plan long-range
strategy, and respond to market shifts.
We’re looking for a founding leader to build this function from the ground up. You will be responsible
for developing the systems, methodologies, and team that give Meesho’s leadership a continuously
updated, outside-in view of the competitive and market landscape — turning fragmented signals into
decision-grade insight.
This is a high-visibility, high-autonomy role. You will work directly with senior leadership and across
category, finance, and product teams. If you’ve spent years building expertise in market estimation,
data triangulation, and insight generation as a consultant or researcher, and you’re now ready to
own a function end-to-end inside a high-growth company — this role was designed for you

lists.content:
• Design and operationalize a multi-source data collection and analysis framework covering the Indian e-commerce and adjacent commerce landscape.
  • Develop standardized methodologies for market estimation, competitive signal triangulation,and financial benchmarking.
  • Create and maintain competitor economic models, including shadow P&L estimation at category level, using published financials, market data, field inputs, and internal benchmarks.
  • Establish structured tracking of international e-commerce peers to identify playbooks,strategic patterns, and early indicators of market shifts relevant to India.
  • Deliver decision-grade competitive inputs to leadership across planning cycles, quarterly reviews, pricing decisions, and investor preparation.
  • Build the connective tissue between external intelligence and internal decision-making —designing output formats, cadences, and dissemination mechanisms that fit how the organization actually makes decisions.
  • Synthesize complex, often ambiguous market signals into crisp, actionable briefs that executives can act on quickly.
  • Own the external benchmarking dimension of strategic planning and long-range forecasting.
  • Hire, mentor, and lead a small, high-caliber team of analysts focused on data collection, modelling, and insight generation.
  • Manage relationships with external data partners, field research agencies, and market consultants.
  • Evaluate and integrate AI-assisted tools and automation into the intelligence workflow

• 7–12 years of professional experience, with a significant portion in market research,management consulting, or strategy roles where competitive analysis, market sizing, and financial estimation were core to your work.
  • You have worked at strategy / market insights practice of a consulting firm (MBB, Kearney)or Nielsen, or comparable market intelligence You deeply understand how market data is collected, how estimates are constructed from incomplete inputs, and how to generate insights from ambiguous signals.
  • Alternatively, you may have built this expertise in-house at an e-commerce, consumer internet, FMCG, or fintech company in a strategy, analytics, or market intelligence function.
  • You understand P&L construction and can think in financial terms — take rates, contribution margins, unit economics, cost structures. You are comfortable building financial models from first principles.
  • You are ready to transition from advisory / research into an operator role where you own the function, build the team, and are accountable for outcomes.
    •  
    Skills & Strengths
  • Estimation & triangulation:You can take multiple imperfect data points and converge on a defensible view. You know which sources to trust, which to cross-reference, and which to discard.
  • Insight generation:You tell decision-makers what it means and what to do about it. You write crisp, structured briefs, not academic papers.
  • Financial modelling:You are comfortable with scenario analysis, sensitivity testing, and building models that accommodate uncertainty.
  • Stakeholder management:You can frame insights for executive audiences, build structured intake with cross-functional teams, and manage external partners and agency relationships.
  • Network:You bring (or can quickly build) an industry network — former colleagues, market consultants, industry contacts — that serves as a legitimate source of market intelligence.
  • High agency — you thrive in ambiguity and can build something from nothing without waiting for perfect inputs or detailed instructions.
  • Intellectual honesty — you frame outputs with explicit confidence levels, distinguish estimates from facts, and never oversell precision.
  • Decision-oriented — you measure your own success by whether your work changes a decision, not by the volume of analysis you produce.
    •

About us 

Welcome to Meesho, where every story begins with a spark of inspiration and a dash of entrepreneurial spirit. We're not just a platform; we're your partner in turning dreams into realities.

Curious about life at Meesho? Explore our Glassdoor - our people have a lot to say and they've helped us become a loved workplace in India.

Our Mission

Democratising internet commerce for everyone — Meesho (Meri Shop) started with a single idea in mind: to be an e-commerce destination for Indian consumers and to enable small businesses to succeed online.

We provide our sellers with benefits such as zero commission and affordable shipping solutions in the market. Today, sellers nationwide are growing their businesses by tapping into Meesho’s large and diverse customer base, state-of-the-art tech infrastructure, and pan-India logistics network through trusted third-party partners.

Affordable, relatable merchandise that mirrors local markets has helped us connect with internet users and serve customers across urban, semi-urban, and rural India. Our unique business model and continuous innovation have established us as a part of India’s e-commerce ecosystem.

Culture and Total Rewards

Our focus is on cultivating a dynamic workplace characterized by high impact and performance excellence. We prioritize a people-centric culture, dedicated to hiring and developing exceptional talent.

Total rewards at Meesho comprise a comprehensive set of elements — monetary, non-monetary, tangible, and intangible. Our 9 guiding principles, or "Mantras," are the backbone of how we operate, influencing everything from recognition and evaluation to growth discussions. Daily rituals and processes like “Problem First Mindset,” “Listen or Die,” our Internal Mobility Program, Talent Reviews, and Continuous Performance Management embody these principles.

We offer competitive compensation — both cash and equity-based — tailored to job roles, individual experience, and skill, along with employee-centric benefits and a supportive work environment. Our holistic wellness program, MeeCare, includes benefits across physical, mental, financial, and social wellness. This includes extensive medical insurance for employees and their families, wellness initiatives like telehealth, wellness events, and fitness-related perks.

To support work-life balance, we offer generous leave policies, parental support, retirement benefits, and learning and development assistance. Through personalized recognition, gratitude for stretched work, and engaging activities, we promote employee delight at the workplace. Additional benefits such as salary advance support, relocation assistance, and flexible benefit plans further enrich the Meesho experience.

At Meesho, we are committed to creating an inclusive and accessible workplace where every individual can thrive. In compliance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, we uphold the following principles:

  • Equal Opportunity: We ensure that employment opportunities are never denied on the grounds of disability if the candidate is otherwise competent to perform the job.

  • Accessible Workplace: Our facilities are designed to be fully accessible, with amenities and assistive devices provided to support differently abled individuals in their work.

  • Inclusive Hiring Process: We adopt a transparent and non-discriminatory selection process, including providing application forms in alternate formats and offering reasonable accommodations during interviews upon request.

  • Career Growth: We provide adequate training post-recruitment and pre-promotion, with training materials available in accessible formats to enable equal career progression.

  • Support & Confidentiality: A dedicated liaison officer/committee addresses concerns and grievances, while maintaining strict confidentiality of disability-related information.

  • Awareness & Inclusion: We conduct awareness programs to promote a culture of inclusivity across the organization.

Meesho welcomes applicants and employees of all abilities and is dedicated to fostering an environment where differently abled persons can achieve their full potential.

Know more about Meesho here : https://www.meesho.io/
descriptionPlain:
About the Team:
At Meesho, we are building a next-generation Business Advisory, Governance & Risk function—one that goes
beyond traditional audit and compliance to actively shape how the business scales sustainably.
This team sits at the intersection of strategy, operations, and risk, working closely with leadership to identify control
gaps, improve processes, create business operation frameworks and unlock efficiencies across the organization. From strengthening governance frameworks to enabling faster, smarter decision-making, we play a critical role in Meesho’s growth journey.
We operate with a first-principles mindset, a bias for action, and deep cross-functional collaboration. Team members
gain exposure across business, product, supply chain, and finance—ensuring a steep learning curve and meaningful
impact. Alongside high performance, we value strong team culture, ownership, and collective success.
About the Role:
This is a high-visibility leadership role with direct exposure to the Audit Committee (AC). You will play a critical
role in strengthening governance by presenting key findings, risk insights, and control frameworks to the Audit
Committee and senior leadership.
The role will report into the CFO & Audit Committee, ensuring independence, objectivity, and strong oversight across Meesho’s risk and control environment. You will not only safeguard the organization but also act as a strategic advisor, influencing decisions that improve efficiency, scalability, and resilience.

gain exposure across business, product, supply chain, and finance—ensuring a steep learning curve and meaningful
impact. Alongside high performance, we value strong team culture, ownership, and collective success.

About the Role:

This is a high-visibility leadership role with direct exposure to the Audit Committee (AC). You will play a critical
role in strengthening governance by presenting key findings, risk insights, and control frameworks to the Audit
Committee and senior leadership.

The role will report into the CFO & Audit Committee, ensuring independence, objectivity, and strong oversight across Meesho’s risk and control environment. You will not only safeguard the organization but also act as a strategic advisor, influencing decisions that improve efficiency, scalability, and resilience.

lists.content:
Lead Enterprise Risk & Governance
  • Drive risk assessment across key business functions including Supply Chain, Seller ecosystem,
    Customer Experience, Marketing, and Technology
  • Build and continuously evolve Meesho’s ERM framework and risk register.
    • Own Internal Audit & Controls
  • Design and execute a risk-based internal audit plan.
  • Strengthen internal controls, identify gaps, and implement scalable solutions.
    • Partner with Business Leaders
  • Work closely with functional heads to identify, prioritize, and mitigate risks.
  • Act as a trusted advisor to leadership on risk-informed decision-making.
    • Drive Process Excellence & Automation
  • Identify opportunities to improve efficiency, reduce leakages, and enhance controls.
  • Lead standardization and automation initiatives across processes.
    • Monitor Emerging Risk Areas
  • Stay ahead of evolving risks including data privacy, cybersecurity, ESG, and regulatory
    compliance.
    • Stakeholder & External Coordination
  • Collaborate with external auditors, legal, and compliance teams as required.
  • Ensure alignment with regulatory requirements and industry best practices.

Experience & Background
  • 8-10 years of experience in business roles, consulting, risk, strategy, or finance.
  • Background in Internal Audit is not mandatory; diverse experiences are valued.
  • Prior exposure to Audit Committee / Board interactions is a plus.
    • Core Capabilities
  • Strong first-principles thinking with deep business acumen.
  • Deep business understanding with the ability to connect risks to outcomes.
  • Ability to operate in ambiguity and build from scratch.
    • Leadership Traits
  • Excellent communication skills, especially with senior leadership and board-level stakeholders.
  • Strong stakeholder management and ability to drive alignment across functions.
  • High ownership, proactive mindset, and bias for action.
    •
